differential_diagnoses: Postpartum Psychosis (ruled out by high level of insight and absence of delusions)
next_steps: Psychoeducation for the patient and spouse explaining ego-dystonic intrusive thoughts in the postpartum period to reduce stigma and fear
Initiate or optimize SSRI therapy (typically at higher therapeutic doses for OCD) with close monitoring for efficacy
Referral to a specialized psychologist or psychiatrist for Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) with Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP)
Avoid unnecessary hospitalization or Child Protective Services reports, as the patient maintains clear insight and lacks intent to harm the infant
Continue monitoring blood pressure and address physical recovery (nipple healing/pain management) to reduce environmental stressors
Establish a safety plan that involves the spouse's support to alleviate the patient's exhaustion and anxiety
Administer standardized rating scales (e.g., EPDS and Y-BOCS) to objectively quantify symptoms and track treatment progress
